item.title: Hello world!
item.dc:creator: admin
item.content:encoded: Welcome to WordPress. This is your first post. Edit or delete it, then start writing!

wp:comment.wp:comment_content: Hi, this is a comment. To get started with moderating, editing, and deleting comments, please visit the Comments screen in the dashboard. Commenter avatars come from Gravatar.

item.title: Sample Page
item.dc:creator: admin
item.content:encoded:
This is an example page. It's different from a blog post because it will stay in one place and will show up in your site navigation (in most themes). Most people start with an About page that introduces them to potential site visitors. It might say something like this:

Hi there! I'm a bike messenger by day, aspiring actor by night, and this is my website. I live in Los Angeles, have a great dog named Jack, and I like piña coladas. (And gettin' caught in the rain.)

...or something like this:

The XYZ Doohickey Company was founded in 1971, and has been providing quality doohickeys to the public ever since. Located in Gotham City, XYZ employs over 2,000 people and does all kinds of awesome things for the Gotham community.

As a new WordPress user, you should go to your dashboard to delete this page and create new pages for your content. Have fun!

item.title: Privacy Policy
item.dc:creator: admin
item.content:encoded:
Who we are

Suggested text: Our website address is: http://localhost:8081.

Comments

Suggested text: When visitors leave comments on the site we collect the data shown in the comments form, and also the visitor’s IP address and browser user agent string to help spam detection.

An anonymized string created from your email address (also called a hash) may be provided to the Gravatar service to see if you are using it. The Gravatar service privacy policy is available here: https://automattic.com/privacy/. After approval of your comment, your profile picture is visible to the public in the context of your comment.

Media

Suggested text: If you upload images to the website, you should avoid uploading images with embedded location data (EXIF GPS) included. Visitors to the website can download and extract any location data from images on the website.

Cookies

Suggested text: If you leave a comment on our site you may opt-in to saving your name, email address and website in cookies. These are for your convenience so that you do not have to fill in your details again when you leave another comment. These cookies will last for one year.

If you visit our login page, we will set a temporary cookie to determine if your browser accepts cookies. This cookie contains no personal data and is discarded when you close your browser.

When you log in, we will also set up several cookies to save your login information and your screen display choices. Login cookies last for two days, and screen options cookies last for a year. If you select "Remember Me", your login will persist for two weeks. If you log out of your account, the login cookies will be removed.

If you edit or publish an article, an additional cookie will be saved in your browser. This cookie includes no personal data and simply indicates the post ID of the article you just edited. It expires after 1 day.

Embedded content from other websites

Suggested text: Articles on this site may include embedded content (e.g. videos, images, articles, etc.). Embedded content from other websites behaves in the exact same way as if the visitor has visited the other website.

These websites may collect data about you, use cookies, embed additional third-party tracking, and monitor your interaction with that embedded content, including tracking your interaction with the embedded content if you have an account and are logged in to that website.

Who we share your data with

Suggested text: If you request a password reset, your IP address will be included in the reset email.

How long we retain your data

Suggested text: If you leave a comment, the comment and its metadata are retained indefinitely. This is so we can recognize and approve any follow-up comments automatically instead of holding them in a moderation queue.

For users that register on our website (if any), we also store the personal information they provide in their user profile. All users can see, edit, or delete their personal information at any time (except they cannot change their username). Website administrators can also see and edit that information.

What rights you have over your data

Suggested text: If you have an account on this site, or have left comments, you can request to receive an exported file of the personal data we hold about you, including any data you have provided to us. You can also request that we erase any personal data we hold about you. This does not include any data we are obliged to keep for administrative, legal, or security purposes.

Where your data is sent

Suggested text: Visitor comments may be checked through an automated spam detection service.

item.title: Phase 1 dogfood
item.content:encoded:
AWT block + pattern showcase

One instance of every shipped block and pattern. Headings label each entry; dividers separate them. UI shell blocks normally live in template parts (header / sidebar / footer); they appear here for completeness and may look out of context.

awt/section

Constrained-width content wrapper with Carbon spacing rhythm and optional background.

Inside an awt/section with backgroundColor "layer-01".

awt/icon

Standalone Carbon icon — decorative by default, accessible name when informative.

awt/button

Carbon button — multiple kinds and sizes.

awt/link

Carbon link variant — distinct from a regular anchor.

awt/list + awt/list-item

Unordered (flat):

Unordered (nested):

Ordered (flat):

Ordered (Carbon-styled, nested): outer level shows "1." counter; nested level switches to lower-latin "a." per Carbon's spec.

awt/tag

Carbon tag — color variants + dismissible (filter) mode.

awt/breadcrumb + awt/breadcrumb-item

Default (with trailing slash):

noTrailingSlash:

awt/menu-button

WAI-ARIA menu-button pattern via floating-ui. Click to open; outside-click / Escape / Tab close.

awt/tooltip

Hover or focus the trigger. Floating-ui flips + shifts at viewport edges.

awt/toggletip

Click-not-hover variant. Opens an anchored popover whose content stays visible until dismissed (Escape, click outside, or click the trigger again).

awt/text-input

awt/password-input

Password field with show/hide visibility toggle. Click the eye to reveal what you typed.

awt/text-area

awt/checkbox

awt/radio-button-group + awt/radio-button

awt/toggle

awt/form

awt/notification

High contrast (default):

Low contrast:

awt/tile

Default tile

A non-interactive content tile.

Clickable tile

Renders as a link.

awt/accordion + awt/accordion-item

Content of the first accordion section.

Single-open mode: opening this closes the others.

Disabled item.

awt/hero

awt/feature-grid

Feature one

Description of feature one.

Feature two

Description of feature two.

Feature three

Description of feature three.

UI shell blocks (normally in template parts)

The blocks below live in parts/header.html, parts/sidebar.html, and parts/footer.html by default. Shown here for completeness; visuals may look out of context.

awt/skip-link

Visually hidden until focused. Tab into it from the address bar to see it.

awt/header-brand

awt/header-nav + awt/header-nav-item

awt/header-global + awt/header-action

awt/color-scheme-toggle

awt/side-nav + awt/side-nav-section + awt/side-nav-link + awt/side-nav-divider

Site chrome: the side nav docks to the left edge of the screen, next to the header, so a live example cannot sit inline on this page. Edit it in the Site Editor, in the "Side navigation" template part.

awt/footer-section + awt/footer-link

Patterns

Pre-composed groups of blocks, surfaced in the Site Editor's pattern inserter under AWT — Sections.

Pattern: awt/hero

Pattern: awt/feature-grid

Why AWT

Accessible

Every block outputs semantically correct HTML and is validated against WCAG 2.2 AA.

Carbon Design System

Proven visual system from IBM, paired light/dark variants out of the box.

Free, forever

GPL-2.0-or-later. No telemetry, no callbacks, no lock-in.

Pattern: awt/faq-accordion

Frequently asked questions

Yes. The theme and the blocks plugin are GPL-2.0-or-later and ship via WordPress.org with no telemetry, no callbacks, and no license keys.

Carbon Design System from IBM. AWT preserves Carbon's class grammar exactly, so anything built against Carbon's CSS works.

Yes. Visitors switch between paired light and dark Carbon variants via the header toggle. Site owners pick the pairing at design time.

End of showcase.

awt/code-snippet

Inline:

Run to compile the plugin.

Single-line:

Multi-line (with copy button):

awt/content-switcher

Segmented control. Arrow keys navigate; click activates. Use for ≤4 short options.

List view — records shown as a vertical list.

Grid view — records shown as cards in a grid.

Timeline view — records shown on a chronological timeline.

awt/data-table

Read-only, sortable, zebra-striped. Click a column header to sort; click again to reverse; third click clears.

awt/dropdown

Carbon-styled single-select (button + listbox via floating-ui). Use when you want the Carbon UI; use awt/select for native OS dropdown behavior.

awt/modal + awt/modal-opener

Click the opener to launch the modal. Escape, backdrop click, or either footer button closes; focus returns to the opener.

This will permanently remove your account and all associated data. This action cannot be undone.

awt/pagination

On archive / blog templates, leave Total pages = 0 and the block reads $wp_query->max_num_pages automatically. Below is a static 12-page example.

awt/select

Native HTML select with Carbon styling. The mobile-friendly version of a single-value picker.

awt/tabs + awt/tab + awt/tab-panel

Specifications panel — second panel content.

Overview panel content. Arrow keys (Left/Right) navigate between tabs and immediately activate the matching panel.

Reviews panel — third panel content.

Frequently asked questions

Install AWT from the WordPress admin search. The free theme and blocks plugin both live on WordPress.org. Activate the theme, then activate the blocks plugin to get every Carbon component as a real block.

AWT runs alongside WooCommerce without breaking. WooCommerce's own templates render with WC default styling at Stage 1. Full Carbon-styled WooCommerce templates are part of AWT Premium.

Yes. The Site Editor's palette panel lets you override any Carbon token. Contrast is checked against both light and dark theme scopes so you cannot accidentally ship an inaccessible palette.

item.title: Navigation
item.dc:creator: admin
item.content:encoded:
<!-- wp:navigation-link {"label":"About Us","type":"page","id":154,"url":"http://localhost:8081/?page_id=154","kind":"post-type","metadata":{"bindings":{"url":{"source":"core/post-data","args":{"field":"link"}}}}} /-->
<!-- wp:navigation-link {"label":"Component showcase","type":"page","id":95,"url":"http://localhost:8081/?page_id=95","kind":"post-type","metadata":{"bindings":{"url":{"source":"core/post-data","args":{"field":"link"}}}}} /-->
<!-- wp:navigation-link {"label":"hdjasdh a khdashh iasjdijdasj klkjkljdasijjj sdjsjds","type":"page","id":303,"url":"http://localhost:8081/?page_id=303","kind":"post-type","metadata":{"bindings":{"url":{"source":"core/post-data","args":{"field":"link"}}}}} /-->
<!-- wp:navigation-link {"label":"Linter demo 01 — Missing image alt text (#1, Error)","type":"page","id":276,"url":"http://localhost:8081/?page_id=276","kind":"post-type","metadata":{"bindings":{"url":{"source":"core/post-data","args":{"field":"link"}}}}} /-->
<!-- wp:navigation-link {"label":"Linter demo 02 — Heading-level skip (#2, Warning)","type":"page","id":277,"url":"http://localhost:8081/?page_id=277","kind":"post-type","metadata":{"bindings":{"url":{"source":"core/post-data","args":{"field":"link"}}}}} /-->
<!-- wp:navigation-link {"label":"Linter demo 04 — Empty image-only link (#4, Error)","type":"page","id":278,"url":"http://localhost:8081/?page_id=278","kind":"post-type","metadata":{"bindings":{"url":{"source":"core/post-data","args":{"field":"link"}}}}} /-->
<!-- wp:navigation-link {"label":"Linter demo 07 — Illogical heading order (#7, Warning)","type":"page","id":280,"url":"http://localhost:8081/?page_id=280","kind":"post-type","metadata":{"bindings":{"url":{"source":"core/post-data","args":{"field":"link"}}}}} /-->
<!-- wp:navigation-link {"label":"Linter demo 16 — Identical link text, different links (#16, Error)","type":"page","id":287,"url":"http://localhost:8081/?page_id=287","kind":"post-type","metadata":{"bindings":{"url":{"source":"core/post-data","args":{"field":"link"}}}}} /-->
<!-- wp:navigation-link {"label":"One pattern at a time","type":"page","id":79,"url":"http://localhost:8081/?page_id=79","kind":"post-type","metadata":{"bindings":{"url":{"source":"core/post-data","args":{"field":"link"}}}}} /-->
<!-- wp:navigation-link {"label":"Pattern showcase","type":"page","id":76,"url":"http://localhost:8081/?page_id=76","kind":"post-type","metadata":{"bindings":{"url":{"source":"core/post-data","args":{"field":"link"}}}}} /-->
<!-- wp:navigation-link {"label":"Pattern smoke — stats-bar inlined","type":"page","id":71,"url":"http://localhost:8081/?page_id=71","kind":"post-type","metadata":{"bindings":{"url":{"source":"core/post-data","args":{"field":"link"}}}}} /-->
<!-- wp:navigation-link {"label":"Phase 1 dogfood","type":"page","id":4,"url":"http://localhost:8081/?page_id=4","kind":"post-type","metadata":{"bindings":{"url":{"source":"core/post-data","args":{"field":"link"}}}}} /-->
<!-- wp:navigation-link {"label":"Sample Page","type":"page","id":2,"url":"http://localhost:8081/?page_id=2","kind":"post-type","metadata":{"bindings":{"url":{"source":"core/post-data","args":{"field":"link"}}}}} /-->
<!-- wp:navigation-link {"label":"test sdasdasdasdasda","type":"page","id":299,"url":"http://localhost:8081/?page_id=299","kind":"post-type","metadata":{"bindings":{"url":{"source":"core/post-data","args":{"field":"link"}}}}} /-->

item.title: Pattern smoke — stats-bar inlined
item.dc:creator: admin
item.content:encoded:
sdsds

sdasdas sdhuhdusd sdjsaidjasi

read more

sdsasad

item.title: Pattern showcase
item.dc:creator: admin
item.content:encoded:
Pattern showcase

Every AWT pattern, inlined for visual review. Each section heading carries the pattern's slug. Use this page to scan compositions; use the editor's pattern inserter to drop a pattern into a real page.

Section patterns

awt/hero — AWT — Hero

awt/feature-grid — AWT — Feature grid (3-up)

Why AWT

Accessible

Every block outputs semantically correct HTML and is validated against WCAG 2.2 AA.

Carbon Design System

Proven visual system from IBM, paired light/dark variants out of the box.

Free, forever

GPL-2.0-or-later. No telemetry, no callbacks, no lock-in.

awt/stats-bar — AWT — Stats bar

awt/testimonial — AWT — Testimonial

awt/pricing-table — AWT — Pricing table

awt/faq-accordion — AWT — FAQ accordion

Frequently asked questions

Yes. The base product is GPL-2.0-or-later and is published on WordPress.org with no telemetry, no callbacks, and no license keys. Premium adds optional capabilities; the free theme and plugin are the complete product.

Carbon Design System from IBM. The theme preserves Carbon's class grammar exactly, so anything built against Carbon's CSS works.

Yes. Visitors switch between paired light and dark Carbon variants via the header toggle. Site owners pick the pairing at design time.

awt/cta-section — AWT — CTA section

Ready to publish accessible by default?

Install the theme and the blocks plugin from WordPress.org. No license keys, no telemetry, no callbacks. The accessibility linter is built in.

awt/newsletter-signup — AWT — Newsletter signup

Stay in the loop

One email per release. New blocks, accessibility-linter changes, and what's coming next. No marketing fluff.

awt/team-grid — AWT — Team grid

Meet the team

Name One

Role and short bio. Replace with real content.

Name Two

Role and short bio. Replace with real content.

Name Three

Role and short bio. Replace with real content.

awt/contact-form — AWT — Contact form

Get in touch

Send us a message. We respond within one business day.

awt/logo-cloud — AWT — Logo cloud

Trusted by teams at

Carbon-derived patterns

awt/carbon-card — AWT — Card (Carbon pattern)

Card heading

Short supporting copy. Cards are containers for related content — group a heading, body, and one optional action.

awt/carbon-dialog — AWT — Dialog (Carbon pattern)

This action will permanently change your account configuration. You can review what changes before confirming.

awt/carbon-disclosure — AWT — Disclosure (Carbon pattern)

The disclosure pattern hides supporting detail behind a click. Use it for content the reader may not need on first scan, like FAQ answers or expanded specifications.

Each disclosure item carries its own keyboard target. Tab navigates between rows; Enter and Space toggle the active row.

Set the parent accordion's singleOpen to true to make this behave like a radio group — only one row open at a time.

awt/carbon-empty-states — AWT — Empty state (Carbon pattern)

No results yet

There's nothing here yet. Create your first item to get started — you can always come back and edit later.

awt/carbon-error-messages — AWT — Error messages (Carbon pattern)

awt/carbon-forms — AWT — Form (Carbon pattern)

Create an account

awt/carbon-login — AWT — Login (Carbon pattern)

Sign in

Forgot password?

Layout templates

awt/layout-one-column-narrow — AWT — Layout: one column (narrow)

Single-column narrow container. Width is capped at 42rem — the Carbon-recommended reading line length for long-form prose.

awt/layout-one-column-contained — AWT — Layout: one column (contained)

Single-column container content. Replace with your blocks.

awt/layout-one-column-full — AWT — Layout: one column (full width)

Full-width container. Useful for hero sections, full-bleed images, and brand statements where vertical rhythm matters more than reading width.

awt/layout-two-columns-equal — AWT — Layout: two columns (50/50)

Left column content.

Right column content.

awt/layout-two-columns-33-66 — AWT — Layout: two columns (33/66)

Sidebar / aside column.

Main content column.

awt/layout-two-columns-66-33 — AWT — Layout: two columns (66/33)

Main content column.

Sidebar / aside column.

awt/layout-two-columns-25-75 — AWT — Layout: two columns (25/75)

Narrow aside.

Main content column.

awt/layout-two-columns-75-25 — AWT — Layout: two columns (75/25)

Main content column.

Narrow aside.

awt/layout-three-columns-equal — AWT — Layout: three columns (equal)

Column 1.

Column 2.

Column 3.

awt/layout-three-columns-25-50-25 — AWT — Layout: three columns (25/50/25)

Left aside.

Center main column.

Right aside.

awt/layout-four-columns-equal — AWT — Layout: four columns (equal)

Col 1.

Col 2.

Col 3.

Col 4.

awt/layout-stacked-sections — AWT — Layout: stacked sections

Section 1

Replace this section with a hero, feature grid, or any other section pattern.

Section 2

Tinted background section — alternates with the previous to create visual rhythm. Replace with a testimonial, pricing table, or stats bar.

Section 3

Final section. End on a CTA, FAQ, or contact form.

Page-level starter templates

awt/page-about — AWT — Page: About

About us

One- or two-paragraph introduction. Who you are, what you do, why it matters. Keep it concise — the team grid below carries the rest.

Optional second paragraph for context: history, mission, philosophy.

awt/page-contact — AWT — Page: Contact

Contact us

Tell us what you need and we'll get back within one business day. For urgent issues, use the support email on the footer.

awt/page-pricing — AWT — Page: Pricing

Pricing

Three editions. Pick the one that fits your team today; upgrade or downgrade any time.

awt/page-docs-index — AWT — Page: Documentation index

Documentation

Pick a topic to get started. Each section is grouped by what you're trying to do — explore by task, not by component.

Getting started

Install, activate, pick a style variation, configure your first pages.

Block reference

Every block, its attributes, and authoring tips. Searchable index.

Patterns

Pre-composed page sections. Drop-in starters for hero, pricing, contact.

awt/page-docs-article — AWT — Page: Documentation article

Article title

One- to two-sentence intro that sets up what this article will cover. Carbon-style lead paragraph.

First section

Body content. Use core/heading level 2 for major sections and level 3 for sub-sections. Use awt/notification for inline callouts.

Second section

Continue the article. For code samples, use the awt/code-snippet block.

awt/page-faq — AWT — Page: FAQ

Frequently asked questions

Common questions about pricing, billing, support, and how the product fits into typical workflows. Can't find what you're looking for? Get in touch.

awt/page-legal — AWT — Page: Legal page shell

Page title

Last updated: Month DD, YYYY

Opening paragraph that frames the page. State the purpose plainly. Avoid legalese where possible.

Section 1

Body content. Repeat the heading + paragraph pattern as needed. Number sections only if the document mandates it (legal contracts typically do, accessibility statements typically don't).

Section 2

More body content.

Contact

How to reach you about this document. Include an email and (where required by law) a postal address.

item.title: One pattern at a time
item.dc:creator: admin
item.content:encoded:
Card heading

Short supporting copy. Cards are containers for related content — group a heading, body, and one optional action.

item.title: Component showcase
item.content:encoded:
Every shipped AWT block rendered with all its variations. Each variation has a labeled sub-heading. Patterns are excluded — see the dogfood page for pattern previews.

awt/section

Constrained-width content wrapper with optional background, theme-scope override, and Carbon spacing rhythm. Foundation for marketing layouts.

maxWidth: content (default 66rem), no background

Constrained-width content wrapper without a background fill.

backgroundColor: layer-01 (light gray)

Section with backgroundColor "layer-01" — Carbon's subtle surface tone.

backgroundColor: layer-02

Section with backgroundColor "layer-02".

maxWidth: narrow (42rem)

Narrow section — for prose-width content.

maxWidth: wide (82.5rem)

Wide section.

themeScope: g100 (forces dark scope inside this section)

Dark-scope section. Carbon tokens resolve to the dark palette inside this island regardless of the page's active theme.

themeScope: dark / light (resolve to the active style variation's paired scopes)

Section with theme scope "Dark (active dark variant)" — renders in the style variation's paired dark scope (g90 or g100).

Section with theme scope "Light (active light variant)" — renders in the style variation's paired light scope (white or g10).

paddingBlock: 09 (3rem) — taller breathing room

Section with extra vertical padding.

awt/inline-set

Carbon-style inline container — wraps button / tag / icon / link children with a consistent gap so authors don't need per-instance margin.

Horizontal, gap-md (default)

Horizontal, gap-sm (4px) — tight inline group

Horizontal, gap-lg (1rem) — relaxed row

Horizontal, align-center

Horizontal, align-between (push to edges)

Vertical orientation

awt/icon

Standalone Carbon icon. Decorative by default (aria-hidden); switch off "Decorative" to provide an accessible name. Uses CSS mask so any Carbon icon paints in the selected color.

Multiple icons (24px, inherit color)

Size 16 / 20 / 24 / 32

Color tokens (support-success / info / warning / error / link-primary)

Informative (carries accessible name; visible to screen readers)

awt/button

Carbon button — 5 kinds × 5 sizes, optional trailing icon, disabled state, and link variant (renders <a> when href is set).

Kinds (primary / secondary / tertiary / ghost / danger)

Sizes (sm / md / lg / xl / 2xl)

With trailing icon

Disabled state

As link (rendered as <a>)

awt/link

Carbon link variant — 3 sizes, optional trailing icon, inline mode, disabled, and visited-state visibility.

Sizes (sm / md / lg)

With trailing icon

Disabled

Visited-state visible

awt/tag

Carbon tag — 10 color tokens × 3 sizes × optional dismissible filter mode.

Color tokens — 10 Carbon tag colors

Sizes (sm / md / lg)

Filter (dismissible)

awt/notification

Carbon notification — 4 kinds (info / success / warning / error) × 2 contrast levels × inline / toast variant.

Kinds × inline (default), high-contrast

Low contrast variant

Toast variant

Hide close button

awt/tile

Carbon tile — content container with 4 variants: default, clickable (link), selectable (checkbox or radio-tile group), expandable (details / summary).

Default (read-only)

Default tile

A non-interactive content tile.

Clickable (renders as <a>, full surface links to /docs)

Clickable tile

Click anywhere — full surface routes to /docs.

Selectable (single tile = checkbox-style toggle; click to flip aria-checked)

Toggle me

Single selectable tile. Click to select / deselect.

Selectable as radio-tile group (3 tiles share groupName; selecting one deselects the others — IBM-style)

Essentials

$2,800/year

Secure data exchange basics.

Standard

$8,400/year

Real-time event notifications.

Premium

Custom

Dedicated success manager.

Expandable (native <details> with Carbon chevron — toggles open/closed)

AWT includes a Carbon-aligned block library + a block theme + a set of starter patterns. The blocks plugin is registered separately from the theme so you can update them independently.

Everything is GPL-2.0-or-later. No telemetry. No license keys.

Expandable, open by default

Authors can set `defaultOpen: true` so the tile loads in the expanded state.

awt/accordion + awt/accordion-item

Carbon accordion — 3 sizes × 2 chevron alignments × optional single-open mode.

Default (md size, end-aligned chevron)

Body for the first section.

Body for the second section.

Body for the third section.

Size sm

Tighter type and padding.

Tighter type and padding.

Size lg

Looser type and padding.

Looser type and padding.

Start-aligned chevron

Disclosure indicator floats inline-start.

Same alignment.

Single-open mode

Opening another closes this one.

Mutually exclusive expansion.

Mutually exclusive expansion.

awt/tabs + awt/tab + awt/tab-panel

Carbon tabs. Tabs pair with panels by DOM ordinal (Nth tab activates Nth panel). Keyboard pattern follows WAI-ARIA automatic activation.

Horizontal (default)

First panel content.

Second panel content.

Third panel content.

With disabled tab

Active panel.

Inactive panel.

Inactive panel.

Inactive panel.

Disabled panel.

Vertical orientation (left rail tabs, right-side panels)

Account panel — manage username, avatar, and profile settings.

Notifications panel — email and in-app preferences.

Billing panel — invoices, payment methods, plan tier.

Security panel — password, 2FA, active sessions.

awt/content-switcher

Carbon segmented control — a tabs-like switcher that holds its own content panels (awt/content-switcher-item + awt/content-switcher-panel). Use for ≤4 short mutually-exclusive options.

Default md, 3 segments — switches the content below

List view — records as a vertical list.

  • Annual report 2024
  • Q3 financials
  • Board minutes

Grid view — records as cards in a grid.

Annual report 2024

Q3 financials

Board minutes

Timeline view — records in chronological order.

  1. Jan — Annual report 2024
  2. Sep — Q3 financials
  3. Nov — Board minutes

2 segments

Day-mode content panel.

Night-mode content panel.

4 segments, size lg

First-quarter figures.

Second-quarter figures.

Third-quarter figures.

Fourth-quarter figures.

Size sm

Panel A.

Panel B.

Panel C.

awt/pagination

Numbered page links with prev / next, ellipsis collapsing, first / last visible.

Current 4 of 12

Current 1 (no prev)

Last page (no next)

Small set (3 pages, no ellipsis)

awt/breadcrumb + awt/breadcrumb-item

Carbon breadcrumb — last item is the current page (no link). Optional `noTrailingSlash` modifier suppresses the slash after the final item.

3 levels (last marked current) — default trailing slash after final item

2 levels

No trailing slash (noTrailingSlash: true)

awt/text-input

Carbon text input — 7 types × 3 sizes × invalid / warning / disabled / readonly states. Inline and fluid layouts.

Default (md size, type text)

Sizes (sm / md / lg)

With helper text

Invalid state

Warning state

Disabled

Readonly

Inline layout

Fluid layout (label inside field)

Type email

Type number

awt/text-area

Carbon multi-line text input. Same state-and-helper system as text-input.

Default

With helper text

Invalid state

Warning state

Disabled

awt/password-input

Text input with visibility toggle. Same state-and-helper system.

Default (with show/hide toggle)

With helper text

Invalid state

Warning state

awt/checkbox

Carbon checkbox — default / checked / indeterminate / disabled / invalid + helper / invalid text.

Default

Checked by default

Indeterminate

Disabled (unchecked)

Disabled (checked)

Invalid

With helper text

awt/radio-button-group + awt/radio-button

Carbon radio group — vertical / horizontal × label-left / label-right × invalid / helper / required.

Vertical orientation

Horizontal orientation

Label position: left

Invalid

With helper text

awt/toggle

Carbon toggle — binary switch with 2 sizes (md / sm), optional disabled / readonly states, custom on/off state labels, and an optional visually-hidden top label. Carbon does not ship a "side label" variant; the top label is the canonical layout.

Default (off)

On by default

Small size (sm) — smaller switch + 10px knob

Small size, on

Disabled (on)

Custom on/off state labels (Yes / No)

hideLabel: true — top label removed from visual flow

The toggle below has the accessible name "Wi-Fi" set on its label, but `hideLabel: true` collapses that label to a 1×1 px clip rectangle so it's announced by screen readers without appearing visually. The "Off" text below the switch is the STATE TEXT (controlled by labelA/labelB), not the label. Compare side-by-side with the default toggles above — they have a visible label sitting on top of the switch; this one does not.

awt/select

Native HTML <select> with Carbon styling. Best for mobile-friendly value picking.

Default (native HTML <select>)

Size sm

Size lg

Invalid

Disabled

awt/dropdown

Carbon JS-powered dropdown. Three sizes × invalid / disabled / helper states.

Default (md)

Size sm

Size lg

With helper text

Invalid

Disabled

awt/modal + awt/modal-opener

Carbon modal — accessible dialog with focus trap, Escape close, backdrop click. Opener pairs with the modal by shared modalId.

Default modal

Body content goes here.

Danger modal

All your data will be permanently deleted.

Large modal

A roomier modal for longer content.

awt/menu-button

Button-triggered dropdown menu. floating-ui handles positioning. Keyboard arrows move focus.

Default

With disabled item

awt/code-snippet

Carbon code-snippet — 3 variants × optional copy button.

Inline (sits in flowing prose)

Run to compile.

Single-line (with copy button)

Multi-line (with copy button)

Hidden copy button

awt/data-table

Carbon data-table — 5 sizes × zebra × sortable × static-width × sticky-header. Per-column cellType: boolean.

Default (size md)

Sortable (click any column header to sort: asc → desc → none cycle)

Set `sortable: true` to make every column header a clickable sort button. Clicks cycle through three states — ascending (▲) → descending (▼) → none (restores original order). `aria-sort` updates on the active column for screen-reader support. Try clicking "Amount" to sort by value, then click again to reverse.

Sortable with pre-sorted default (defaultSortKey: amount, defaultSortDirection: desc) — table renders already sorted

Boolean cell type (checkmark / em-dash)

Small size

X-large size (xl) — designed for multi-line cell content

XL rows are 4rem (64px) tall with top-aligned text. Carbon's spec uses this density for cells with longer descriptive content (often a primary line plus a secondary line below). Single-line content in an XL row leaves a lot of empty space — use md or lg for plain text tables.

awt/list + awt/list-item

Carbon-styled list — ordered / unordered, flat or nested.

Unordered (flat)

First item

Second item

Third item

Ordered (flat)

First step

Second step

Third step

awt/hero

Marketing hero — eyebrow, heading, description, optional CTAs (inner buttons), and text-with-image-right layout.

Eyebrow + heading + description + CTA buttons

Without eyebrow

awt/feature-grid

CSS grid of feature tiles. Columns 2 / 3 / 4.

3 columns (default)

Accessible

WCAG 2.2 AA across every block.

Carbon-based

Proven visual system from IBM.

Free, forever

GPL-2.0-or-later. No telemetry.

2 columns

Two-up A

Body for the first tile.

Two-up B

Body for the second tile.

4 columns

Four A

A.

Four B

B.

Four C

C.

Four D

D.

awt/stat

Marketing primitive for big-number stats — 76px display value, supporting heading, optional description.

Number + heading + description

Compact (no description)

Text value (e.g., "Zero")

awt/testimonial

Quote + attribution — 4 mark styles, 3 quote sizes, 2 attribution layouts, 2 kinds, 2 alignments.

Default (double-curved mark, large quote)

Mark style: double-straight

Mark style: single-curved

Mark style: none

Card kind (layer-01 background)

Inline attribution

Centered alignment

awt/pricing-tile

Pricing-comparison tile — tier name, price, period suffix, description, CTA. Optional featured + badge + selectable.

Three tiers, side by side

awt/faq-item

FAQ block — renders the same accordion-item HTML structure but contributes to a Schema.org FAQPage JSON-LD block.

Default

Yes — GPL-2.0-or-later. No telemetry, no callbacks, no license keys.

Different heading level (h4)

IBM Carbon. AWT preserves Carbon's class grammar exactly.

Another FAQ entry

Yes. Visitors switch between paired light / dark Carbon variants via the header toggle.

awt/tooltip

Carbon tooltip — small descriptive label revealed on hover / focus of the wrapped trigger.

Default (hover / focus to reveal)

Align: bottom

awt/toggletip

Click-persistent tooltip — for longer content with markup. Carbon's reference behavior.

Default (click trigger reveals popover)

awt/form

Form wrapper — adds Carbon's cds--form class + form-item spacing rhythm, optional legend / description.

Default (legend + inputs + submit)

Without legend

UI shell components

Header / sidebar / footer / skip-link / color-scheme-toggle and the side-nav family normally live inside template parts (header.html, footer.html, sidebar.html). Configure via Site Editor → Template parts. Components: awt/skip-link, awt/header-brand, awt/header-nav, awt/header-nav-item, awt/header-global, awt/header-action, awt/color-scheme-toggle, awt/side-nav, awt/side-nav-section, awt/side-nav-link, awt/side-nav-divider, awt/footer-section, awt/footer-link.

item.title: header
item.dc:creator: admin
item.content:encoded:
<!-- wp:group {"tagName":"header","className":"cds--header","layout":{"type":"flex","flexWrap":"nowrap","justifyContent":"space-between"},"style":{"spacing":{"padding":{"top":"0","right":"0","bottom":"0","left":"0"}}}} --> <header class="wp-block-group cds--header" style="padding-top:0;padding-right:0;padding-bottom:0;padding-left:0"><!-- wp:awt/skip-link /-->
<!-- wp:awt/header-brand /-->
<!-- wp:awt/header-nav {"ariaLabel":"Primary"} --> <!-- wp:awt/header-menu {"text":"Product"} --> <!-- wp:awt/header-nav-item {"text":"Features","href":"#"} /--> <!-- wp:awt/header-nav-item {"text":"Integrations","href":"#"} /--> <!-- wp:awt/header-nav-item {"text":"Updates","href":"#"} /--> <!-- /wp:awt/header-menu --> <!-- wp:awt/header-nav-item {"text":"Pricing","href":"#"} /--> <!-- wp:awt/header-nav-item {"text":"Customers","href":"#"} /--> <!-- /wp:awt/header-nav -->
<!-- wp:awt/header-global --> <!-- wp:awt/button {"text":"Get started","kind":"primary","size":"md","className":"awt-hide-on-mobile"} /--> <!-- wp:awt/color-scheme-toggle {"kind":"icon-only"} /-->
<!-- wp:awt/header-action {"iconName":"search","label":"Search","href":"/?s="} /-->
<!-- wp:awt/header-action {"iconName":"notification","label":"Notifications","href":"/"} /-->
<!-- wp:awt/header-action {"iconName":"user--avatar","label":"Account","href":"/wp-login.php"} /--> <!-- /wp:awt/header-global --></header> <!-- /wp:group -->

item.title: Linter demo 01 — Missing image alt text (#1, Error)
item.dc:creator: admin
item.content:encoded: Should flag 1 Error: an image with empty alt text.a

item.title: Linter demo 02 — Heading-level skip (#2, Warning)
item.dc:creator: admin
item.content:encoded:
Should flag 1 Error: heading jumps H2 to H4.

Section

Subsection (skipped H3)

item.title: Linter demo 04 — Empty image-only link (#4, Error)
item.dc:creator: admin
item.content:encoded: Should flag 1 Error: a linked image with no alt text.

item.title: Linter demo 05 — Non-descriptive link text (#5, Warning)
item.dc:creator: admin
item.content:encoded:
Should flag 1 Warning: link text “Read more”.

Read more

item.title: Linter demo 07 — Illogical heading order (#7, Warning)
item.dc:creator: admin
item.content:encoded:
Should flag 1 Warning: an H2 placed directly under a deeper H4 (illogical heading order).

A deep subsection (H4)

A new top-level section (H2) placed right after

item.title: Linter demo 10 — Line breaks instead of paragraphs (#10, Warning)
item.dc:creator: admin
item.content:encoded:
Should flag 1 Warning: an empty line forced with consecutive line breaks.

First paragraph of text.

Second block of text, separated by an empty line made with line breaks.

item.title: Linter demo 11 — Color outside the palette (#11, Warning)
item.dc:creator: admin
item.content:encoded:
Should flag 1 Warning: a custom (non-palette) text color.

This paragraph uses a custom hex color.

item.title: Linter demo 12 — Low color contrast (#12, Error)
item.dc:creator: admin
item.content:encoded:
Should flag 1 Error: white text on a yellow palette background.

White text on a yellow background — fails contrast.

item.title: Linter demo 13 — Table without header cells (#13, Warning)
item.dc:creator: admin
item.content:encoded: Should flag 1 Warning: a table with no header row.

item.title: Linter demo 14 — Inline SVG with no accessible name (#14, Warning)
item.dc:creator: admin
item.content:encoded: Should flag 1 Warning: an inline SVG with no title/aria-label.

item.title: Linter demo 15 — Alt text from file name (#15, Error)
item.dc:creator: admin
item.content:encoded: Should flag 1 Error: alt text that echoes the file name.

item.title: Linter demo 16 — Identical link text, different links (#16, Error)
item.dc:creator: admin
item.content:encoded:
Should flag 1 Error: two “Our report” links to different URLs.

Our report
Our report
